observations.recommendation:
1.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the creation of the new
Inventory Committee. Likewise, issue an Order mandating the –
a) Municipal Engineer to:
i. Conduct an inspection and inventory of the road networks, making
sure to segregate each road asset component, with information as to
its location and actual condition.
ii. Prepare the inventory report, adding therein the assessed values, and
copy furnish the COA, the Municipal Accountant for purposes of
reconciliation of records.
b) Municipal Assessor to:
i. Assist the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer during the conduct of
inspection/inventory of the road lot components of all road networks
within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.
ii. Assess the cost of the road network/lot components, assigning such
values to the items identified during the inventory as owned and
controlled by the municipality.
iii. Provide the Municipal Engineer with the assessed values on the road
network/lot components identified, for the preparation of the
inventory report.
c) General Service Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network
Property Cards based on the details obtained from the inventory report
furnished by the Municipal Engineer.

1.17. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Start the reconciliation of the records based on the inventory report
furnished by the Municipal Engineer.
b) Maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and subsidiary
ledgers with complete description of each road asset components, and
disclose such information in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years,
pursuant to the requirements under the details prescribed in COA
Circular Nos. 2015-008 and 2015-009.

2.18 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
entries to:
a) correct any erroneous depreciation charges that have resulted in negative
net book values and stop the depreciation of fully depreciated assets.
b) reclassify the amount of ₱1,945,634.37 from the PPE account to the
appropriate Semi-Expendable Property Inventory account and ensure that
reclassified items already issued to end-users are properly supported with
Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS). Henceforth,
record acquisitions below ₱50,000.00 on an individual item basis in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2022-004 and COA Circular No. 2024-006, applying the
prescribed accounting treatment for inventory items; and
c) correct the misclassified PPE items totaling ₱554,611.63 to their proper
PPE accounts to ensure the accuracy and fair presentation of the affected
balances in the financial statements.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the depreciation expense of
₱765,769.75 for completed projects which should have been computed
from the date the assets became operational. Henceforth, the
Construction in Progress account shall be closed to the appropriate
PPE account as soon as the project is completed and the asset becomes
available for use, to allow for the recognition of the corresponding
depreciation expense.
b. record progress billings and payments for materials and labor for
infrastructure projects using the Construction in Progress account, and
reclassify it to the appropriate asset account only upon completion; and
c. review the Lapsing Schedule and ensure that correct depreciation is
computed based on the total project cost, from the actual date the assets
were completed and available for use.

4.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry
to reclassify the installed automatic flood control siren costing ₱2,490,000.00 and the
food and non-food stock pile valued at ₱997,925.00 to the Disaster Response and
Rescue Equipment account and Welfare Goods for Distribution account,
respectively.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Trace and reconcile the records between the electronic system and the
accounting records to determine the amount of taxes payable from prior years
up to the current year;
b. Establish and maintain separate subsidiary records for each type of tax to
facilitate monitoring and timely reconciliation of withheld balances.

6.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit all identified
unremitted taxes, including DST collections, to the BIR. Henceforth ensure
that all taxes due and payable are withheld and remitted in full and, following
the rates and periods prescribed by the BIR regulations, to prevent the accrual
of interest, penalties, and other legal liabilities.

7.11 We recommended that the Chairperson of the GFPS-TWG:
a. conduct gender analysis of the attributed PPAs through the
administration of the HGDG tool during the project identification and
preparation of GAD AR to determine whether the attributed GAD PPAs
entirely address gender related issues, and submit the corresponding
scores; and
b. establish, enhance, strengthen and maintain a gender responsive
evaluation system to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund books for the 20% DF, supported with a separate set of subsidiary ledgers for each development project charged to the said Fund, whether current or continuing, in order to effectively monitor existing fund balances, appropriations and utilizations.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer seek the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, through a resolution, to open a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund to be maintained in the General Fund for easy monitoring of the Fund and to safeguard it from possible misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: (a) transfer the balance of the 20% Development Fund, as computed by the Budget Officer and Accountant, to the new depository account; and (b) transfer monthly to its new depository bank account for 20% DF, the amount equivalent to 20% of the IRA share received by the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer comply promptly with the submission of the three cash advance vouchers and supporting documents together with the required liquidation documents.

observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry
on the overstated depreciation expense amounting to ₱1,172,868.70; provide
residual values when computing depreciation; and compute the depreciation
expense for the Slaughterhouses in order to present the correct valuation of
the properties owned by the Municipality.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer request for the
enactment of an Ordinance to transfer to Trust Fund books and utilize in CY
2024 the unexpended LDRRMF balances from CY 2019 to 2023 totaling
₱24,105,077.29 which were retained in the General Fund.

2.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

c) Pursue the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan to authorize the LGU to
open a bank account solely for the DRRMF Special Trust Fund; and

d) Transfer the cash equivalent to the unexpended balance of the QRF and
MF-MOOE totaling ₱24,105,077.29 immediately upon approval of the
separate bank account for the Special Trust Fund; and to make such
transfer of unexpended balance of the QRF and MF-MOOE annually
before the last working day of each year.

2.15 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

c) Record the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE balances totaling
₱24,105,077.29 for CY 2019 to 2023, and every end of each year thereafter,
under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books; and

d) Notify the Municipal Treasurer, in writing, of the amount of unexpended
QRF and MF- MOOE before the last working day of each year as basis in
making the deposit to the DRRMF Special Trust Fund.

2.16 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the DRRM
Officer, submit to the MDRRM Council for approval, a revised LDRRMFIP
of CY 2024 to include the unexpended LDRRMF balances totaling
₱24,105,077.29 and its planned projects and activities, once the transfer to the
Special Trust Fund is granted with SB authority.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions
totaling ₱ 44,768,888.40 in the General Journal using the prescribed format as
instructed in the Manual and submit a copy to the Audit Team. Henceforth,
use the prescribed format in the preparation of the General Journal for
transactions in the ensuing years, and follow the instructions in the Forms
provided in the MNGAS for LGUs.

4.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, update the Annual Investment Plan during the budget
preparation stage, and include only in the AIP priority projects which are
procurement-ready that can be implemented during the current budget year.

4.12 We further recommended that the Chairman and members of the Bids and
Awards Committee, adopt the “early procurement process” on infrastructure
projects in case there is delay in the approval of the LGU’s annual budget; and
start the bidding process in accordance with the timetable indicated in the AIP
to ensure completion of the projects before the end of the year.

4.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct a thorough
detailed engineering and prepare the Program of Work (POW) and other
technical documents of all projects to be implemented in a particular year
PRIOR to its inclusion in the AIP and update the same when necessary.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer compute the allocation
for Confidential Fund at an amount not exceeding 30% of the total amount
allocated for activities solely devoted for Peace and Order Program only.

5.13 We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor obtain and approve the
cash advance for Confidential Fund only when the required supporting
documents are complete, including the Physical and Financial Plan and the
PNP Certification which should be prepared PRIOR to allocating CF from the
budget for Peace and Order Programs to justify the need for such allocation.

5.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

c.) Submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers of each cash
advance for Confidential Fund, with complete supporting documents, in a
sealed envelope, within seven (7) days from release of check, together with
the Transmittal Letter enumerating the supporting documents, for post
audit; and

d.) Submit directly to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit
(ICFAU) the Liquidation Report (LR) for CF duly signed and approved by
the Municipal Mayor as the Special Disbursing Officer, together with the
supporting documents of the LR with a covering transmittal letter in a
sealed envelope with a visible label “CONFIDENTIAL – For ICFAU
Only”.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a written directive to the
Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Audit Team all the lacking
supporting documents for the grant and disbursements of P14,405,000.00 for
social pension of indigent senior citizen; and to seek the advice of the source
agency regarding the unused amount of P1,116,000.00.
We recommended that Municipal Accountant revert the unsupported and long-outstanding payables by making the necessary accounting entry to adjust the books of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, include in the Annual Investment Plan only those projects that can be procured and implemented immediately during the budget year and monitor strictly the progress of its implementation until their completion within the target dates.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer resolve issues during the detailed engineering phase and implement projects within the contract period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

The Municipal Accountant compute, and deduct from their claims, the liquidated damages for late deliveries to prevent suppliers from neglecting their responsibility to comply with the conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

1.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Appoint a Municipal General Services Officer to take custody of, and be
accountable for all properties, real or personal, owned by the local
government unit
b. Issue an Office Order creating the Inventory Committee and include in its
directive the following Instructions:
i. Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit
Team after the same has been approved by the Municipal Mayor but
before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
ii. Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical count
of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in
accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other relevant
issuance; and
iii. Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit the
same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Audit Team for
verification.
1.15 We also recommended that the Property Officer prepare and maintain
Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its
components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively.
1.16 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Maintain the PPELC for each class of PPE, and LRNLC for each local
road, and reconcile these with the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively;
and
b. Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures in
COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Submit hard copies of the BRS from January to December 2021 duly
supported with copies of bank statements or bank snapshots, cancelled
and paid checks, bank debit and credit memos to facilitate the
verification of the monthly BRS.
b) Henceforth, prepare all the BRS for each bank account maintained
with its authorized depository banks and submit to the Audit Team
within (10) days for receipt after the end of each month; and
c) Prepare and maintain a separate subsidiary ledgers for each bank
accounts maintained.

3.14 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) maintain a separate subsidiary record for each program/project to be
implemented by the Municipality;
b) submit to the Audit Team and Source Agency the: (i) monthly report
on the utilization of existing fund transfer; (ii) liquidation reports of
implemented programs/projects
c) reconcile the balances of each fund transfers made by the Source
Agency specifically those which remained unliquidated in the SA’s
books but a corresponding liquidation report was already submitted.
d) secure a certification from the Source Agency’s Municipal Accountant
for those FTs with submitted liquidation reports;
e) henceforth, closely monitor the submission of the liquidation reports
and always maintain copies of each report submitted to the Source
Agency.

4.10 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
a) Obtain from the Leyte Provincial Health Office the actual information
on the unit cost and quantities of the vaccines received by the
Municipality in relation to its vaccination program;
b) Provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from
the LPHO relative to the receipt of the vaccines, including the data on
the doses administered at the end of each month.
4.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the
perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received by the Municipality and the
doses dispensed, using as reference the report/documents submitted by the
MHO.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
d. trace the transactions that transpired in prior years and compute the
unexpended LDRRM Fund as of December 31, 2021, and every yearend
thereafter, taking into consideration ALL disbursements paid and the
payables recorded in the books of accounts charged to the fund;
e. prepare the LDRRM Fund Utilization Report containing the details of ALL
disbursements paid during the month and furnish a copy to the Municipal
Budget Office for reconciliation and adjustment of the amount obligated in
the RAO; and
f. prepare the Disbursement Voucher, with the necessary supporting
documents, to transfer to the Special Trust Fund, the total unused QRF and
MF-MOOE for CY 2021 and every end of each year thereafter, and record
the same under the account “Trust Liability –DRRMF account.
5.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
a. make the necessary adjustments in the RAO-LDRRMF at yearend based
on the LDRRM Fund Utilization Report of the Municipal Accountant
and transfer the unexpended amount to the Local DRRM Fund – Special
Trust Fund, to avoid the reversion of the MOOE balance to the surplus of
the General Fund at the end of the year; and
b. request for the enactment of an Ordinance to make available the unexpended
appropriation of CY 2021 LDRRM Fund – MOOE, which were reverted to
the surplus of the General Fund, PRIOR TO the payment in CY 2022 of
valid obligations against the LDRRM Fund.

6.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements, the details of the LDRRM Fund, specifically the
composition of the appropriations, amounts utilized and the balances of the
unexpended LDRRM Fund per year.

7.7 We recommended that the Mayor and the MDRRM Office optimize the
utilization of its MDRRM Fund in order to strengthen its capacity to prevent
and mitigate disaster risks and avoid potential damages during disasters.

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor approve the utilization of
MDRRM Fund only for expenses intended for disaster risk reduction and
management activities and discontinue the use of MDRRM Fund for the
payment of regular operating expenses.
8.10 We likewise recommended the MDRRM Officer include in the succeeding
year’s MDRRM Fund Investment Plan the projects and activities which are
for disaster risk reduction and management activities only.

10.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all
Department Heads to indicate in their respective Purchase Requests the
complete details and specifications of the items being requisitioned as guide in
obtaining price quotations.

10.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the detailed
Instructions on the preparation of the Subsidiary Ledgers and supply the
needed information or details for each transaction recorded therein for
facilitative review and analysis of the breakdown of the control accounts.

11.7 We recommended that the Revenue Collecting Clerk henceforth monitor the
effectivity and expiration date of his bond and cause the submission of the
required documents to the Municipal Treasurer for the renewal of the bond
before its expiration.
11.8 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) instruct the Revenue Collection Clerk to postpone all collection activities
until his fidelity bond is renewed;
b) facilitate the renewal of the bond of Mr. Luderico O. Ubaldo and send a
copy of the Confirmation Letter to the Audit Team.

13. Prepare the 2021 Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) based on SAAOB submitted by the Budget Officer, and comply with IPSAS 24 on the proper presentation of budget information in the Financial Statements to facilitate its verification.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the Property Officer in the Municipality
prepare and submit a report on the issuances and distribution of the inventory
items in order that a correct balance for both inventory asset and supplies
expense may be established. Henceforth, record purchases of inventory first
as an asset, and subsequently, as an expense, only when these inventories are
sold, consumed or distributed as required in PPSAS 12.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the cause of
the discrepancy or difference between the reciprocal accounts Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds and adjust the books accordingly; and
henceforth, conduct a periodic reconciliation of reciprocal accounts.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes
to the Financial Statements the necessary information for PPE account that
may be relevant for decision-making of stakeholders.

4.11 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly comply with
the submission of the three cash advance vouchers and supporting documents
together with the required liquidation documents, pursuant to the provisions of
Sections 39(1 & 3) and 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997. We further recommended that management monitor the
submission of liquidation/settlement documents of all cash advances granted to
the same accountable officer during CY 2019.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exert effort to locate
and retrieve the accountable forms. Specifically:
c) Secure a document or certification from the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office of LGU-Tanauan signifying the receipt of the AF 57 released
to them; and
d) Send a demand letter to the Barangay Treasurers, who were
designated as deputized collectors on the Community Tax Certificate,
instructing them to remit their collections to the MTO and submit a
duly accomplished Report of Collections and Remittances (RCR),
copy furnished the audit team.
The above recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Charge resulting from post-audit of the transactions.

6.7 We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of using
the RATA as payment for the monthly loan amortizations of their employees
and strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget
Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

alternative modes of Procurement above P50,000.00 be posted in the

adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR specifically on the
observance of the recommended timetable relative to the prescribed period of
action on procurement activities to enable timely delivery of goods and
services as well as the benefits by which the intended beneficiaries can derive
therefrom.

9.8 We recommended that Management, in cases involving lump sum
appropriations encroached in general/generic terms, should seek a separate
SB appropriation ordinance/resolution to serve as an authority to enter into
contract in behalf of the Municipal Government, in accordance to Section 22
(c) of RA No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA
audit team.
